skill.name: buddy-sings
skill.description: Creates personalized songs for a Claude Code buddy by using the pet profile, optional session context, MiniMax music generation, and local audio playback.
skill.summary: Turn a Claude Code buddy profile into a personalized generated song.
skill.author: MiniMax-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, filesystem, network, and weak-crypto patterns, but most command and weak-crypto hits are Markdown examples or text false positives. The confirmed risk is privacy exposure: the skill reads Claude companion and memory files and can send derived personal context to MiniMax through mmx music generation.

medium_findings.title: Broad Claude Memory Access
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to automatically scan Claude companion data and memory files for personal context. This is legitimate for personalization, but it can expose private user history if the scope is wider than expected.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly instructs reading ~/.claude.json and scanning Claude memory paths. The purpose is stated as song personalization, so the risk is privacy exposure rather than confirmed abuse.

medium_findings.title: Personal Context Sent to External Music Service
medium_findings.description: The generated lyrics and prompt can include gathered personal context, then the skill calls mmx music generation. That likely sends this content to an external MiniMax service after user confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions connect local context gathering to a generated prompt and lyrics, then invoke mmx music generate. The preview and confirmation reduce risk, but external processing remains clear.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Construction With Dynamic Text
medium_findings.description: The command examples place dynamic prompt, lyrics, name, and filename values into shell-style CLI arguments. If implemented through a shell without careful escaping, unusual input could break argument boundaries.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are examples, not executable code in this repository. The risk depends on how an agent runs them, so this is a moderate implementation concern rather than confirmed command injection.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Setup and Playback Commands Are Mostly Benign
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged many Markdown command examples. Install, authentication, git-log, and playback commands are expected for this music workflow and are mostly hardcoded.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible documentation examples with expected tools and no hidden payload. Some still execute local programs, so they remain low-risk operational evidence.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Hits Are Text False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography alerts appear to match ordinary prose, Markdown, and music-generation wording rather than cryptographic APIs or algorithms. No evidence of cryptographic code was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md is an instruction document with no implementation of MD5, SHA1, DES, or similar algorithms. The cited areas contain descriptive text and templates, not crypto operations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hidden Claude Profile and Cache File Access
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill reads ~/.claude.json and writes voice cache data under ~/.claude/skills. Hidden-file access is expected for the buddy feature but should be disclosed to users.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file paths are explicitly listed and are central to the skill behavior. This is a real filesystem pattern with a bounded functional purpose.

dangerous_patterns.title: Automatic Memory Search
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill tells the agent to search Claude project memory files automatically when personal context is implied. This can collect sensitive details before the user has reviewed the final song prompt.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The instruction uses automatic scanning language and names the memory locations. The privacy concern is direct and does not depend on static pattern interpretation.

dangerous_patterns.title: External CLI-Based Generation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill invokes mmx music generate with generated prompt and lyrics content. This creates an external-processing dependency for potentially personal text.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The generation command is explicit and uses the required mmx CLI. The exact network behavior is outside this file, but the CLI is presented as a MiniMax generation tool.

content.user_title: Create Songs for Your Claude Code Buddy
content.value_statement: Users want their Claude Code buddy to feel personal, not generic. This skill builds a consistent pet voice, creates original lyrics, generates music with MiniMax, and plays the result locally.
actual_capabilities: Reads the Claude Code buddy companion profile from the local Claude configuration.
Creates and caches a consistent vocal identity from the pet name and personality.
Gathers optional session, memory, or git context for personalized song themes.
Writes original pet-perspective lyrics or uses the MiniMax lyrics optimizer.
Calls mmx music generation and saves an MP3 under the user music folder.
Detects common local audio players and attempts playback after generation.
limitations: Requires the mmx CLI, MiniMax authentication, and at least one supported audio player for full use.
Reads Claude configuration and memory paths, so users should avoid sensitive themes when privacy matters.
Generated music quality depends on the external MiniMax service and prompt interpretation.
The skill provides instructions only; it does not include a standalone application or bundled audio engine.

use_cases.title: Personal Buddy Theme Song
use_cases.description: Create a recurring song style that matches a user's Claude Code buddy profile.
use_cases.target_user: Claude Code users with an adopted buddy

use_cases.title: Workday Recap Song
use_cases.description: Turn recent project activity or session context into a short musical recap.
use_cases.target_user: Developers who want playful project summaries

use_cases.title: Localized Companion Music
use_cases.description: Generate lyrics and user-facing prompts in the user's language while keeping music prompts optimized.

prompt_templates.title: Sing a Simple Song
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with no extra context.
prompt_templates.prompt: Ask my Claude Code buddy to sing a gentle song about a rainy afternoon.

prompt_templates.title: Use My Buddy Personality
prompt_templates.scenario: Personalized song based on the pet profile.
prompt_templates.prompt: Make my buddy sing something that matches its personality and keeps the voice consistent.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Work Recap
prompt_templates.scenario: Use recent session or git context for lyrics.
prompt_templates.prompt: Have my buddy sing about what I worked on today, then preview the full lyrics before generating.

prompt_templates.title: Regenerate Style and Lyrics
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced revision with a specific creative direction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Change my buddy's voice, make the song upbeat city pop, and rewrite the lyrics from the pet's first-person view.

output_examples.input: My buddy should sing a cozy song about waiting for me after work.
output_examples.output: The assistant finds the buddy profile, describes a warm vocal identity, previews original pet-perspective lyrics, and asks for confirmation before generation.

output_examples.input: Make a song about what I did today.
output_examples.output: The assistant gathers recent session or memory context, creates a workday theme, previews the complete lyrics, and generates a saved MP3 after approval.

output_examples.input: Regenerate the voice and make it more energetic.
output_examples.output: The assistant clears the cached voice identity, builds a new energetic vocal style, and prepares a revised music prompt for confirmation.

best_practices: Review the full lyrics and prompt preview before approving generation.
Use standalone themes when you do not want memory or project context included.
Keep pet names and requested song themes free of shell metacharacters when running CLI commands.
anti_patterns: Do not ask the skill to include private secrets, credentials, or confidential project details in lyrics.
Do not skip confirmation when memory-derived context is used for a song.
Do not reuse copyrighted lyrics or ask the pet to reproduce an existing song.

faq.question: Does this skill require a Claude Code buddy?
faq.answer: Yes. It reads the companion field from the local Claude configuration and stops if no buddy exists.

faq.question: Does it create real audio?
faq.answer: Yes, when the mmx CLI is installed and authenticated. The generated song is saved as an MP3 file.

faq.question: Can it use my recent work as a theme?
faq.answer: Yes. It can inspect session context, Claude memory files, and recent git history when the request implies personal context.

faq.question: Is my local memory data sent outside my machine?
faq.answer: The skill can include derived memory context in lyrics or prompts sent through mmx music generation. Review previews carefully.

faq.question: Can it sing in languages other than English?
faq.answer: Yes. It detects the user language and can write lyrics and user-facing messages in that language.

faq.question: Can I change the pet voice?
faq.answer: Yes. Asking to change or regenerate the voice clears the cached identity and creates a new one.
